maulgar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS landed well below the Top 100 sample (1,274 vs. 1,970 avg/1,930 median) and overheal (59.4%) pushed past even the sample's single worst parse (53.4%), yet the exact-match percentile still came back at 86th - a genuinely fast, 37-second kill leaves little room for HPS separation, so the percentile here reflects the encounter's own short window more than a strong overall performance.
maulgar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom alone accounted for 99.4% of all healing this kill, a 45.7-point gap over the Top 100 sample's 53.7% average share - on a fight this short, a single fast-refreshing HoT dominating the log is expected, not a real rotation gap.
maulgar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Neither Nature's Swiftness nor Swiftmend was cast, undercutting the Top 100 sample's 58% and 77% usage rates respectively - both are plausible omissions on a kill this brief, where a full-cast emergency heal often isn't needed before the boss is already dead.
maulgar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (100% vs. 97.6% Top 100 average) and top-target concentration (27.6% vs. 46.3% average, well below the norm) both point to healing spread evenly across all 5 raid members present, rather than concentrated on one focus target.

gruul.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (650) sat well below the Top 100 average (1,300) and median (1,259), landing at the 59th percentile, and overheal (56.7%) is close to the sample's single worst parse (58.5%) - the weakest efficiency combination of the raid night's early kills, though HPM (4.51 vs. 6.3 avg) also runs well under the norm, suggesting mana conservation is part of the story alongside the overheal.
gruul.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom carried a real but modest 9.2-point larger share than the Top 100 sample (46.2% vs. 37%), one of the smallest composition gaps of the raid night (just behind Morogrim's) - this kill's spell mix tracks the benchmark closer than most.
gruul.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Swiftmend went uncast against an 86%-used Top 100 norm (avg. 2.4 casts), and Mana Potion also went unused against a 55%-used norm - Innervate (on Churbert) and a self-target Nature's Swiftness were both used in line with the sample. Rebirth was cast once, on Vinnyvoss, but the nearest logged cooldown to that death (Innervate, roughly 34 seconds later) came after the death rather than answering it directly.
gruul.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (83.2% vs. 68.1% average, +15.1) and top-target concentration (54% vs. 36.1% average, +17.9) both run well above the Top 100 norm - healing reached more of the raid than typical, but also leaned unusually heavily on a single focus target.

hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: This is the lowest percentile of the raid night (25th) - HPS (510) came in at less than half the Top 100 average (1,269) and overheal (70.5%) exceeds even the sample's single worst parse (58%), a combination that stands out even against an otherwise below-average night.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share (51.7%) ran 10.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average (41%) - a real but middling gap, smaller than several other kills tonight.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Staubz, a real overcast relative to the Top 100 sample (only 10% of parses use it here) - but it's worth flagging as a positive: both real deaths this kill (Staubz, then Deepbowlz roughly a minute later) have another cooldown landing within 6 seconds afterward (Innervate, then Swiftmend), suggesting an active, reactive response even though neither death was itself prevented.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (92.5% vs. 83.5% average) ran ahead of the norm, while top-target concentration (37.5% vs. 48.1% average) ran below it - healing reached more of the raid than typical without leaning heavily on any one target.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (834) came in well below the Top 100 average (1,515), and overheal (61.9%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (54.3%) - with 9 real deaths logged on this kill (the most of any boss tonight), the below-average HPS likely reflects a raid that was struggling to stay alive rather than a healing-output problem in isolation.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation's share (11%) ran 9.4 points behind the Top 100 average (19.5%), the boss's real composition gap - Lifebloom, by contrast, tracked close to the sample (13.8% vs. 17.8%).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: All 9 deaths this kill happened after the raid's only Swiftmend cast (13 to 50 seconds later, in sequence) - none of them had a cooldown answering them directly, and Rebirth was never cast despite 9 real deaths on the log. This is the clearest cooldown-coverage gap of the raid night, even though the Top 100 sample itself only uses Rebirth on 26% of parses here.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (60.5% vs. 50.5% average) and top-target concentration (14.2% vs. 15.1% average) both track close to the Top 100 norm - with 22 distinct targets touched, the widest spread of any boss tonight, consistent with a chaotic, high-death fight.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,039) landed below the Top 100 average (1,830), and overheal (53.9%) exceeds even the sample's single worst parse (46.5%) - both real signals that this kill ran less efficiently than the benchmark population, even at a middling 53rd percentile.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share (55.8%) ran 17.4 points ahead of the Top 100 average (38.4%), one of the larger composition gaps of the raid night.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Staubz, a real overcast against the Top 100 sample's 11% usage rate here - but the second death this kill, Zuggnuggs, happened just 1 second after a Swiftmend cast, close enough that the heal likely hadn't landed or wasn't enough to save them. Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, and Mana Potion were all used in line with the benchmark.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (69.3% vs. 73.3% average) and top-target concentration (25.8% vs. 33.9% average) both track slightly below the Top 100 norm, with 18 distinct targets touched - a spread-out kill without a strong single-target focus.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (935) ran below the Top 100 average (1,442), and overheal (53.9%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (51.1%) - the 71st percentile here comes despite that below-average HPS, similar to the pattern seen on Maulgar.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation's share ran 6.5 points behind the Top 100 average, a modest gap - Lifebloom's share (42.9%) tracked much closer to the sample (37.1%) than on several other kills tonight.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: All 3 deaths this kill (Shlicktree, then Deepbowlz and Vinnyvoss together roughly 40 seconds later) happened 9-11 seconds before the next Swiftmend cast landed - none were directly answered, and Rebirth was never cast despite the 3 real deaths. Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, and Mana Potion were all used in line with the Top 100 sample.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (68.2% vs. 67.3% average) and top-target concentration (42.6% vs. 38.5% average) both track close to the Top 100 norm - a fairly typical target-distribution profile for this boss.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (710) landed well below the Top 100 average (1,602), and overheal (51.5%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (49.1%) - this kill had zero deaths, so the efficiency gap here reflects overhealing rather than a raid-survival problem.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth's share (58.3%) ran 19.3 points ahead of the Top 100 average (39%), a real and sizeable composition gap - and Regrowth's real mana cost (675, the most expensive of the tracked spells) makes this a genuinely mana-heavy healing profile for this kill specifically.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here (Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, Swiftmend, Mana Potion) was used in line with the Top 100 sample - no real deviation on this kill.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (73.6% vs. 56.7% average, +16.9) ran well ahead of the Top 100 norm, with top-target concentration (33.9% vs. 25.2% average) also modestly elevated - healing reached more of the raid than typical here.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (610) and overheal (54.9%, past the sample's worst parse of 53.5%) both look weak against the Top 100 sample here, but the deciding factor is a real self-death: Danceswtrees died mid-fight (one of 6 total deaths this kill), directly cutting off healing output for whatever time remained afterward - the raw efficiency numbers alone understate what's actually a survivability story, not a rotation problem.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share (68.7%) ran 22.6 points ahead of the Top 100 average (46.1%), one of the larger composition gaps of the raid night (behind only Maulgar's, Solarian's, and Kael'thas's) - consistent with a kill cut short by the self-death above, where a low-maintenance HoT would naturally dominate whatever healing window was actually available.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Captinspanky about 4.5 seconds after their death - but roughly 6 seconds after that Rebirth cast, Danceswtrees's own death happened, with no further cooldown available afterward. Of the 6 real deaths this kill, only Captinspanky's was answered; Vajomee, Purehentai, Staubz, and Deepbowlz all died 38-59 seconds away from the nearest logged cooldown (a Mana Potion, not a save-oriented one).
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (31.6% vs. 21% average, +10.6) ran above the Top 100 norm, while coverage (60.6% vs. 50.8% average) tracked close to typical - with 21 distinct targets touched and a self-death partway through, the healing window here was real but cut shorter than a full kill.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (736) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,072), and overheal (59.8%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (61.1%) - yet this kill still ranked at the 85th percentile, similar to the Maulgar pattern where percentile and a below-average HPS coexist. Zero deaths this kill, so the overheal reflects genuine excess healing rather than a fight cut short.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share (65.6%) ran 15.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average (49.9%) - a real but middling gap for this raid night.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here (Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, 4 Swiftmends including one self-cast, and 2 Mana Potions) was used in line with the Top 100 sample - no real deviation on this kill.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (92.9% vs. 79% average, +13.9) and top-target concentration (54.3% vs. 43.6% average, +10.7) both ran above the Top 100 norm - healing reached nearly the whole raid while still leaning more heavily on a top target than typical.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,423) ran below the Top 100 average (2,458), and overheal (36.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (33%) - both real gaps, though less severe in absolute overheal terms than most other kills tonight.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share (22.9%) ran 18.3 points ahead of the Top 100 sample, which barely uses it at all here (4.6% average) - Regrowth remained the dominant spell for both (70.8% here vs. 75.1% average), so this is a real but secondary compositional difference, not a wholesale rotation swap.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Swiftmend went uncast against an 80%-used Top 100 norm (avg. 2.3 casts) - the clearest cooldown gap on this kill. Both real deaths (Captinspanky, then Staubz about 15 seconds later) happened within 20 seconds of the same Innervate cast, though Innervate isn't a save-oriented cooldown, so neither death looks directly answered by it.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (52% vs. 37.2% average, +14.8) ran well above the Top 100 norm, while top-target concentration (13.8% vs. 9.4% average) tracked close to typical - healing reached more of the raid than usual without concentrating unusually on one target.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (664) ran below the Top 100 average (1,224), yet this kill still landed at the 85th percentile - the same pattern seen on Maulgar and Alar. Overheal (56.3%) is the one boss tonight where the number stays within the Top 100 range entirely (best 28.2%, worst 64.7%), rather than exceeding it.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth's share (96.9%) ran 38 points ahead of the Top 100 average (58.9%) - the second-largest composition gap of the raid night, behind only Maulgar's near-total Lifebloom concentration. Lifebloom was never cast this kill at all, against a sample average of roughly 17% combined across its two ranks - a real, complete rotation swap toward Regrowth on this particular boss, not a partial gap.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Swiftmend went uncast against a 90%-used Top 100 norm (avg. 2.4 casts). Rebirth was cast on Captinspanky, the kill's only death - a real overcast relative to the Top 100 sample (9% usage here), and a direct answer to that death even though a Mana Potion cast landed marginally closer in time.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (52.3% vs. 52.7% average) and top-target concentration (16.4% vs. 16.4% average) both matched the Top 100 norm almost exactly - the closest target-distribution match to the benchmark of any boss tonight.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (512) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,014), and overheal (72.8%) is the highest of any boss tonight, exceeding the sample's single worst parse (61.7%). With 6 real deaths logged, the 30th percentile here reflects a rough kill more broadly, not an isolated healing issue.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share (69.5%) ran 28.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average (40.8%), one of the largest composition gaps of the raid night.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was never cast despite 6 real deaths this kill - a real undercast against the Top 100 sample, where 62% of parses use it (avg. 0.6 casts). None of the 6 deaths show a clean cooldown-timing correlation: the closest, Staubz, happened just 74 milliseconds from an Innervate cast, but Innervate doesn't itself heal or protect a target, so this reads as coincidental timing rather than a missed or successful save.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (74.7% vs. 58.4% average, +16.3) ran well above the Top 100 norm - with 23 distinct targets touched, the widest spread of the raid night, consistent with 6 real deaths across a long, difficult kill.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: consistent, aside from two likely-benign single-kill gear swaps (MainHand, OffHand)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: One real gear gap stands out independent of the kill-to-kill swaps: the Back slot (item 28765) carries no permanent enchant across every kill this raid night - a cheap, static enchant that would apply immediately and doesn't require any fight-specific loadout choice. OffHand carries a non-weapon Held-In-Off-Hand item (an orb) this raid, which cannot take a permanent enchant in this era regardless of what's equipped there - not a real gap.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Hydross the Unstable (25th percentile), Kael'thas Sunstrider (30th), and The Lurker Below (39th) are this raid night's lowest-percentile kills and the clearest priorities for a full breakdown. Hydross pairs the raid's lowest raw HPS with overheal exceeding the Top 100 sample's worst parse; Kael'thas and Leotheras the Blind (9 real deaths, the most of the night) point to survivability rather than a single rotation gap. Every percentile shown here is matched to this exact report and fight ID, not a duplicate-upload fallback.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Danceswtrees died mid-fight on Lady Vashj - the raid's only self-death recorded this raid night, cutting off healing output for whatever remained of that encounter afterward. See the Vashj page for the full death-timeline breakdown.

GearCheckItems.Description: MainHand and OffHand vary on a minority of kills
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Both slots differ from the baseline loadout on exactly one kill each, not a lasting swap - consistent with a fight-specific choice rather than a gearing gap.

GearCheckItems.Description: Elixir of Healing Power (Battle) and Elixir of Draenic Wisdom (Guardian) both active on all 12 kills, plus Well Fed food buff
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, complete consumable setup all raid night - the Battle + Guardian Elixir pairing is a fully valid alternative to a Flask under the real TBC rule. Confirmed via a real re-pull (2026-07-15) after fixing two pull-script bugs: the original Flask-vs-elixir classification mislabeled Elixir of Draenic Wisdom as a flask, and a second bug used the wrong combat-log buff name for Elixir of Healing Power (logged simply as "Healing Power", not "Elixir of Healing Power") - caught only because the player's own real memory of her loadout didn't match what the data first showed.
